prerequisites: A Retail Express by Maropost account, and an API Key requested from the client you wish to integrate with. Each API Key is unique to a given client and only provides access to that client's data.
A start_date value: the date from which the connector begins syncing records.

authMethods.name: API Key (x-api-key header, exchanged for a Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the single secret field api_key. The Retail Express API key is sent in the x-api-key header to the authentication token endpoint (https://api.retailexpress.com.au/v2/auth/token), which returns a short-lived Bearer access token that expires after 60 minutes. Subsequent API calls send both Authorization: Bearer <access_token> and x-api-key: <api_key>. The connector manages this token exchange and refresh internally; you only supply the api_key.

setupSteps.title: Obtain a Retail Express API Key
setupSteps.body: Request an API Key from the Retail Express client you wish to integrate with. Each API Key is unique to a given client and only provides access to that client's data.

setupSteps.title: Configure the api_key field
setupSteps.body: Provide the API key as the api_key field in the connector configuration. This is the only secret the connector needs; it uses the key in the x-api-key header to request a short-lived Bearer access token and to authenticate all data requests against https://api.retailexpress.com.au.

setupSteps.title: Set the start_date field
setupSteps.body: Provide the required start_date. The connector uses this date as the point from which to begin syncing records from the Retail Express API.

sources.title: Getting Started with the Retail Express API (Developer Portal)
